ABSTRACT
Artificial intelligence with virtual reality and the overwhelming digitalization in the next 10 years will
change the role of healthcare marketing from managing to creation of new health. The dynamic of digital
force development and the disruptive technological jump to high competitiveness of healthcare provid-
ers will make the role of marketing as the core. The functionality of healthcare and its advantages with
professional medical manipulations and diagnosis will relay of modern marketing function to flourish the
preventive and active healthcare for everyone to bring the health manipulations to the home of patients
and make health the daily substance, utilizing deepest digitalization of relation to patients, insurers,
health providers, controllers, and to the new health quality systems into one big, branded family. All this
innovative development is today under construction, and they rely on healthcare marketing. The chapter
includes the evolution of healthcare marketing from the beginning to advanced stages and the future of
healthcare marketing development.



1. INTRODUCTION

We live in a period of disruptive technologies, especially in such an innovative market what is Health
Care. Healthcare is established not only by the market but by the Country too, which tries to win in the
peaceful competition against some other countries (Gigauri & Djakeli, 2021). Healthcare marketing is
a phenomenon with having long history, started with the first outdoor ads, banners, and city walls, en-
graved or written, sometimes found by archeologists in ancient cities like Pompey, or seen by tourists,
glancing at the walls of rare retained taverns in Old Western Europe (Berkowitz,2021). This is the time

DOI: 10.4018/979-8-3693-0679-6.ch001



       Copyright © 2024, IGI Global. Copying or distributing in print or electronic forms without written permission of IGI Global is prohibited.
                                                                                                          ﻿
                                                                                      Healthcare Marketing




that we can entitle as Marketing before Marketing, in which, Health Elites, sacredly retain their medical
wisdom and give knowledge of Medicine only to their family members, trying to hold the Profession
of a physician in scarcity as something alike the premium brand. In recent years, healthcare marketing
has increasingly focused on personalization and data-driven approaches. Healthcare organizations use
patient data to tailor marketing messages, offer personalized health recommendations, and improve
patient engagement. Modern healthcare marketing prioritizes patient-centric approaches, focusing on
improving patient experiences and engagement. Content marketing, patient testimonials, and educational
materials are used to empower patients to make informed decisions about their healthcare. One study
suggests that physicians according to marketing, are divided into very interesting segments: a) rule-
bound doctors, b) high flyers, c) skeptical experimenters, d) selective majority, and e) conservators. This
segmentation mirrors the readiness of some segments to participate in marketing-based and innovative
healthcare with clinical trials (Spielmans & Parry, 2010). Even this understanding shows that marketing
implementation made clinics more entrepreneurial. The importance of healthcare marketing is growing
and as some researchers claim, from evidence-based medicine, companies turned to marketing-based
medicine. Now healthcare fully using the concept of marketing, turning the marketing instruments into
healthcare ones, but what was the past?


2. THE HISTORICAL STAGES OF HEALTHCARE MARKETING

The use of marketing in the healthcare industry can be divided into some relevant stages.

a)   before the 20th century stage can be named the early healthcare marketing period. In this timeframe,
     the primitive forms of word-of-mouth communications were very relevant marketing instruments,
     that could be used well in the rural or urban healthcare business. In this period clinics and hospi-
     tals, doctors, and nurses were assessed by aperture of reputation. As we know reputation is a time-
     consuming business and can be achieved through a hardworking process, via awareness and image.
     At the same time, the awareness of the health organization, doctor, or nurse must be category-based.
     This type of awareness is called recall. If a doctor, nurse, hospital, or clinic is immediately recalled
     by the patient having a category need for healthcare, then this is strong awareness, which leads to
     a positive image and good reputation. About the use of advertising in healthcare and especially in
     pharmacy, there is historical evidence engraved on the walls of old cities. Especially interesting are
     the first newspapers advertising some medicaments. It can be said that European pharmaceutical
     ads had their origins in the 16th - 17th centuries, especially in the first newspapers. But how can we
     say that this was marketing if the word marketing only appeared in 1910? Can be signs of marketing
     before marketing? Logically not, but advertising what now is the part of marketing concept itself
     is much older than marketing. So we can emphasize that signs of marketing in healthcare appeared
     before conceptualization of the marketing sciences (Thomas,2008).
b)   because the USA was and remains as a classical marketing country the modern healthcare market-
     ing started there, but with complications. In 1950s marketing was already popular among various
     industries, but in healthcare it was adopted only in retail and supplier sectors. Until 1980 marketing
     in healthcare was only promotion concept. Some hospitals in the USA already had departments
     of marketing but the encouraging point for all nonprofit healthcare organizations was the famous
     article of Philip Kotler entitled as Marketing for Nonprofit Organizations (Kotler,1975). This ar-


2
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




     ticle energized the use of marketing into health. In 1977 appeared a new Book: Marketing health
     care, written by MacStravic (MacStravic, 1977). In 1980 appeared one interesting scientific study,
     about consumers of healthcare services (Berkowitz & Flexner,1980). During 1980s the role of
     marketing grew in healthcare organizations. Having segmented their patients, organized marketing
     budget, differentiated their healthcare products and services for patients, targeting customers and
     promoting the health services – some innovative hospitals and clinics became more competitive.
     The Society for Healthcare Strategy & Market Development was established. The academic dis-
     cipline the Healthcare Marketing in 80s was adopted by many academic programs of healthcare
     administration at universities. In 60s the growing role of electronic media increased the relevance
     of communication to patient, what emphasized the professional use of public relation. In 1970s
     the communities as a customers of healthcare became under focus of hospitals and all their com-
     munication activities were direct to communities. In 80s healthcare markets became buyer markets,
     the role of marketing research grew with this issue. The direct to consumer relationship marketing
     was under demand. The invention and development of Diagnostic Related Grouping model in the
     late sixties at Yale University was the marketing type of innovation into healthcare management
     as well (Averill, et al., 1998).
c)   In 2000s not only healthcare but also healthcare management and marketing methods became
     already adopted by many health organizations. The countries reforming their health sector, espe-
     cially in post-soviet peace, being struggling to turn into new, market driven healthcare, had a lot of
     problems (Djakeli,2013). Even some countries tried to adopt social insurance healthcare models,
     then the same countries, turned to market driven, liberal and libertarian models of health financ-
     ing, encouraged by works of famous American economist Milton Friedman (Friedman,1982). The
     reform policy and the disasters happened to those countries made some researchers to think that
     either healthcare, or healthcare financing reforms, must be backed by solid healthcare marketing
     research, with great role of segmentation, targeting, differentiation and positioning (Djakeli, 2023).
     The ideas of Milton Friedman and some other liberal economists energized the use of full concept
     of marketing into health.
d)   The exploding power of social media embarrassing the healthcare and the prospect and current
     patients became one of mainstreams in healthcare marketing of 2010 with the patient engagement
     and experiential marketing.
e)   The Digital Revolution challenged old healthcare market and the focus on Empowered Patient,
     Telemedicine and virtual healthcare started. The internet enabled healthcare providers to have an
     online presence through websites, social media, and online advertisements. Patients could now
     research healthcare options and access information more easily. With advancements in technology,
     patients could receive medical consultations, diagnoses, and treatment remotely through video calls
     and online platforms. Personalization and Data-Driven Marketing has great role to empower the
     patient.

    The total spending for healthcare marketing and advertisement was growing. From 1996 to 2017, the
USA healthcare marketing grew fast. The promotional learning campaigns for different health services,
drugs, disease and laboratory testing increased up from $17 billion to more than $29 billion. The market-
ers of healthcare were using sharpest type of marketing approaches known as direct-to-consumer (DTC)
advertising. The share of Direct to consumer ads in healthcare in above mentioned timeframe increased




                                                                                                         3
                                                                                                           ﻿
                                                                                       Healthcare Marketing




from 2 billion to more than 9 billion USD in 2017. The same approach was used by pharmacy marketers
and the DTCs Also increased from $1.3 billion to $6 billion” in America (Schwartz & Woloshin,2019).
    Health service marketing was slowly moving to current indicators. As we know that 20 years ago
healthcare ads were banned by American Marketing Association, but American Hospital Association
destroyed this ban and ads started regularly. When we speak about Advertising we know that this is not
full marketing but, if advertisement in planned by marketers and health administrators, that means that
they work on full marketing program, segmenting population to find with whom their message must
speak, differentiating the health services to show unique advantages, targeting the selected segment
of patients and referrals for better impact and position their brands for long term advantages. So that
means that when world started the advertising of health services, it started the cycle of marketing what
is unchangeable for any health company, because of rules and experiences. Some marketing approaches
in the advertisements have were directed to fear and then hope without emphasizing the methods of the
treatment, in cancer clinics for example (Schwartz & Woloshin, 2019). The fourth industrial revolution
created better chances for any type of manufacturers to be integrated with Internet of Things (IoT). This
revolutionizing approaches affected the healthcare, establishing Healthcare 4.0. henceforth also “HC4.0”
(Aceto, et al., 2020).


3. THE DIFFERENCE BETWEEN HEALTH MARKETING
AND HEALTHCARE MARKETING

The difference between health marketing and healthcare marketing is obvious, health marketing is
defined as a creator and communicator, and a deliverer of health information using consumer-centered
and scientific methods to protect and widespread the health for all (Bernhardt, 2006), in the case than
healthcare marketing is trying to deal with customer choice, attract and retain customers, work for cus-
tomer satisfaction and loyalty, like any other service industry, with one exclusion, healthcare is dealing
with destiny, health and life and also the continuation of the life, possible death and death avoidance
(Corbin et al., 2001). So health marketing is more about great healthy life style and prevention from the
possible health related risks, than healthcare marketing is the process organizer for entire healthcare
service industry with financiers, insurers, healthcare policy makers, health providers and controllers.
    Healthcare is just a technology market, where consumers like hospitals pay huge money for prestigious
and competitive equipment (Thimbleby, 2013). Health marketing is defined as combination of realities
from marketing of the life sciences, social marketing and marketing of health services (Crié & Chebat,
2013). Sometimes the discussions are about the fact that patients are not regular customers and health is
not a good. The study of healthcare marketing has vital objects, the establishment, delivery, promotion
and consumption of healthcare services, which are urgent, according the knowledge of customers. Not
providing those services what are expected by customers are lethal either in long term or immediately.
Customers and patients of health frequently are co-producers of the health. The demand for personaliza-
tion of healthcare services and even customization is growing and marketing plays here the great role.
Environmental changes have great influence on marketing. For example, the COVID-19 has changed
many approaches and methods, and made healthcare marketers to digitize (Gigauri & Djakeli, 2021b).
    Healthcare marketing for modern digital era is the process what creates needs and predicts wants of
patients, insurers, financiers, state funds, controllers, regulators, on the wave of technological inventions,
not only in the medical organizations but also among financiers, closely working with digital channels


4
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




making the health financing more smart and easy. In any process the digitalization will bring a lot of
innovations, what will blur the borders between healthcare providing and financing, controlling and
periodical market researches.
    The old concepts of healthcare marketing were emphasizing that this is a process of understanding the
needs and wants of a target market (Cooper, 1994). The purpose of healthcare marketing was understood
as some viewpoint about how to integrate health organization, its analysis, management functions and
control it in the process of healthcare delivery. This was old view. The digital world will do all these
parts in the synchronized way.
    The modern healthcare marketing can be defined as a process by which healthcare organizations
either create new customers, and engage them to build strong mass customized relationship to create
healthcare value and receive the value from customers in exchange. Accordingly, 44% of the marketing
costs for healthcare products and services will be dedicated to mobile and digital platforms and this is
the future we embrace (Purcarea, 2019). The healthcare is regulated business, that’s why the way of
health reforms and the philosophy of health of the country emphasizes not only the competitiveness of
the system but its actors (Djakeli, 2013).


4. THE MAIN GOALS OF HEALTHCARE MARKETING

Several goals can be differentiated from those what is seen upon healthcare marketing in the digital era:

•    The reduction of information asymmetry on healthcare market and Increasing Awareness, Image
     and Reputation of Healthcare Providers, financiers, insurers, state policy makers, pharmacy
     stakeholders, medical associations, doctors and nurses: Healthcare marketing is aiming to raise
     awareness, image and reputation of patients, prospect patients about medical services, health-
     care facilities, treatment options, insurers, financiers, state policies, best cases, the empowering
     patient-clinic engagements and health-related issues among the target audience. By increasing
     awareness, positive images and strong reputation and via digitalization, healthcare organizations
     can attract, educate, retain, empower current and potential patients worldwide.
•    Patient Acquisition: One of the primary goals of healthcare marketing is to attract new patients or
     customers to healthcare providers, hospitals, clinics, pharmaceutical companies, and other health-
     care organizations. By increasing patient acquisition, healthcare providers can grow their patient
     base and revenue.
•    Enable Provider-Patient Communication: Digital tools facilitate direct communication between
     patients and healthcare providers, allowing patients to schedule appointments, receive test results,
     and ask questions conveniently.
•    patient empowerment, what means the personalization of healthcare for the patient, education of
     patient, enabling patients to participate in the process of self-healthcare, train patient, remotely
     monitoring the patient, controlling the systems for the better results of care, improving the condi-
     tions of self-care and comfortability for patients.
•    Retention: Healthcare marketing also focuses on retaining existing patients and customers.
     Satisfied and loyal patients are more likely to return for future healthcare needs and recommend
     the services to others, contributing to long-term success.




                                                                                                       5
                                                                                                       ﻿
                                                                                   Healthcare Marketing




•    Brand Building: Building a strong and positive brand image is crucial in healthcare marketing.
     A well-established brand creates trust challenges strong credibility among potential patients and
     customers, leading to increased patient loyalty and the Strategic Competitive Advantage (SCA)
     on the market.
•    long term Educating of Patients and prospect patients: Healthcare marketing aims to educate
     patients and the general public about health conditions, preventive care, treatment options, and
     overall health and wellness. Informed patients are more likely to make better healthcare decisions
     and take proactive steps in managing their health.
•    Promoting Preventive Care and health life style: Healthcare marketing campaigns often empha-
     size preventive care and healthy behaviors to encourage individuals to take a proactive approach to
     their health. Promoting preventive care can lead to better health outcomes and reduced healthcare
     costs in the long run.
•    Supporting Patient Adherence: Marketing efforts can play a role in promoting medication adher-
     ence and treatment compliance among patients. By providing information and reminders, health-
     care organizations can improve patient outcomes and the effectiveness of treatments.
•    Community Engagement: Healthcare marketing often involves engaging with the local commu-
     nity to address specific health concerns, promote health-related events or initiatives, and foster a
     sense of trust and support.
•    Enhancing Reputation and Trust: Healthcare marketing endeavors to enhance the reputation and
     credibility of healthcare providers and organizations. Positive patient testimonials, high-quality
     services, and patient satisfaction are vital for building trust within the community.
•    Adapting to Market Changes: In a rapidly evolving healthcare landscape, marketing efforts need
     to be flexible and adaptable. Healthcare marketing must respond to changes in patient preferences,
     advancements in technology, and shifts in the regulatory environment.
•    Supporting New Services and Technologies: When healthcare providers introduce new services or
     technologies, marketing plays a crucial role in informing the target audience about these innova-
     tions and highlighting their benefits.
•    Strengthening the ethical principles of health: Healthcare marketers must adhere to strict ethical
     guidelines and patient privacy regulations when conducting digital marketing campaigns.


5. THE NEEDS AND WANTS OF CUSTOMERS

Some disturbance in health and wellbeing is a need for healthcare services (Donabedian, 1974). The
healthcare need must be confirmed either by professional healthcare organization or healthcare worker
during medical intervention. A need defined as something like a tension in organism, mostly directed
to addiction to alcohol or heroin, must be ignored, to understand the need for healthiness and healthcare
(Gustavsson, 2014). Needs can be divided into basic and category needs. Sometimes basic needs called
as dispositional ones. The need to healthiness can be understood as basic, but the need for beauty smile
is category oriented. For example, if someone has the category need for healthy, pretty smile, what
means the beautifulness of lips with healthiness and whiteness of teeth, then this person easily converts
this need into want and makes selections among dentist saloons for the best choice, what turns the want
into demand. The notion of healthcare need is also characterized by gap between actual state of health




6
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




and its desired condition. Need in health can be divided into strongly healthcare related needs and those
what are directed to quality of life.
    The structure of needs well describes some unusual character of Healthcare Marketing. The humani-
tarian and realistic models of needs in health, emphasizes the fact that needs are supported by resources
what must be equivalent for services.
    In the Model of Donabedian, it is described two approaches, humanitarian and realistic, to the defini-
tion of the need for health care (Acheson, 1978).
    The Humanitarian Approach describes the logical flow of the model like this: humanitarian ap-
proach is considering the need in harmony to service equivalence of needs, what is linked to services
and the services are dependent from resources themselves.
    The realistic approach describes the logical flow model like this: realistic approach is considering
the need in harmony to Resources what must be equivalent to services and what is linking the service
equivalence of resources to the needs.
    What customers wants from healthcare? The wants of patients can be divided either to the healthcare
system or to their condition. Generally, to say patients want a healthcare system that responds when its
needed (Detsky, 2011). Patients already know the concepts of preventive medicine. Well-informed patients
want more preventive medicine like cancer screening programs that will prevent the terrible illnesses.
But still patients more relay on factual immediate medicine than to preventive. The digital healthcare
will be disruptive, bringing more preventive medical manipulations to the home of the healthcare cus-
tomer, making prevention a lot of diseases, using the healthier lifestyle, genetically oriented preventive
medicine, and many other interesting things, backed by medical Artificial Intelligence.
    Most innovative health labs and research organizations try to find the unmet healthcare needs and
create the new wants for final users (Agatemor et al., 2018). Either the disorders of physical health and
mental health, societal problems or the degree of health-related knowledge of society, culture, traditions,
social groups and family, together with the knowledge of individual is forming the health need (Stanley
et al., 2016). Many residents in many countries are underserved by healthcare and for those who are
in this category, the state does some special offerings. For example, in the USA, the community-based
organizations called as Federally Qualified Health Centers (FQHC) try to study unmet needs and provide
healthcare for underserved American populations. Such centers investigate and describe the underserved
population’s perceptions, beliefs and practices that impact them in the process of healthcare utilization
(Pieh-Holder et al., 2012).
    What is healthcare customer’s desire? Their desires and wants can be divided into some aspects:

a)   patients desire access to healthcare and if they do not procrastinate they want the care soon!
b)   Patients want and desire empathy, kindness, and respect, especially to their privacy!
c)   Patients want no pain by the treatment!
d)   Patients desire more hope!
e)   Patients want more options to be well!
f)   Patients does not like uncertainty about the diagnoses and they want certainty!
g)   Patients and their family members feel guilty if they don’t fight for health and don’t try to be better!
h)   Patients frequently want not to know about the possible near finish of their life!
i)   Patients desire continuity and choice among methods of healthcare!
j)   Patients desire friendly relationships with health professionals!




                                                                                                           7
                                                                                                            ﻿
                                                                                        Healthcare Marketing




k)   Patients want their healthcare team to discuss their health conditions and communicate for better
     decisions in healthcare!
l)   Patients want private, comfortable rooms during hospitalization
m)   If digital monitoring systems afford them to stay home, patients want to stay home and be under
     professional systems of Patient Digital Monitoring!
n)   Patients don’t like out of pocket payment, that’s why they desire some financial systems paying for
     their health costs!
o)   Patients want to know that medical team treating them is the best!
p)   Patients want persuasive testimonials about high quality of the healthcare brand and the team treat-
     ing them!
q)   Patients want reliable information about many other patients, who are now healthy from the same
     sickness they had!
r)   People perceive healthcare as interaction with their doctor and they want their doctor to listen well,
     discuss their problems clearly, be attentive (Wen & Tucker, 2015).

    We know that only patients are not customers of healthcare systems and organizations. There are
institutions, like financiers, insurers, state, patient and medical associations, country and state regulators,
policy makers, law makers and so on. Accordingly, they want:

a)   Effectiveness and Efficiency of healthcare,
b)   Positive indicators about healthcare,
c)   New disruptive and innovative healthcare methodologies and products improving the healthcare,
d)   Social stability and positive view to healthcare systems,
e)   More healthcare providers making healthcare as a buyer’s market (Detsky, 2011).

   The healthcare providers want to refer their patients easily to other providers, they want more clear
and detailed protocols, professional trainings and meeting, transfer of knowledge and easy access to the
new knowledge (Learmonth et al., 2018).


6. MARKETING MIX FOR MODERN HEALTHCARE

Marketing mix concept can be very beneficial to manage healthcare organizations in digital age. Some
period ago researchers were arguing that marketing mix variables could not easily fit health (Crié, et al.,
2013), but now informational revolution and the digitalization made health services to be more flexible
in delivery, what made place to be movable. The same changes we see in product and the service of
Healthcare, the concept of the levels of the product can be as useful in healthcare as in any other catego-
ries. The pricing varies according the strategy of healthcare organization. The promotion is relevant and
must be managed well to develop the positive awareness of healthcare unit, health provider and turning
this awareness firstly into strong image of the organization and after that develop the reputation, what is
achieved by strong healthcare branding stages. But are the four P-s enough? The healthcare marketing
practice shows that the 7-P-s concept will add some relevant views to marketing management, improv-
ing the stamina of health workers.




8
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




6.1. Product/Service

Healthcare products are loosely defined as services (France & Grover,1992). There are product lines and
products mixes in healthcare. Healthcare products/services can be divided into levels, like:

•    Core level of the product or service, something like a level where the functional product is done
     by physicians, technology, doctors and nurses, by telemedicine or artificial intelligence in modern
     healthcare. The core level of the product frequently regulated by some guidelines, having issued
     by national health systems of the countries;
•    The actual level of the product/service, has many components, like a price, brand, design, place,
     shape, relationship to patient, payment and delivery methodology and popularity. The actual level
     of the healthcare product/service can be differentiated by marketing, because it has big opportu-
     nity to achieve product differentiation on this level of the healthcare business.
•    The expected level of the product/service has many components as well. This level is created by
     education of patients and prospect patients. In proper healthcare marketing cycle, expectations
     must be managed by marketing department of the hospital and not by the gossips of the street.
     The real, actual knowledge about the healthcare product/service delivered by special healthcare
     marketing team and controlled after that, is the effective instrument for the creation of expected
     product.
•    The perspective product makes healthcare more interesting and attractive. Every human tries to
     find the continuation especially in healthcare. Through referrals or independently if we have good
     doctor, great nurse, well organized clinic, thus we have not only current but perspective product
     increasing our safety and good mood in the life.
•    The augmented product combines everything to one effective healthcare marketing with guaran-
     tees or warranties of implant, results of diagnose, the outcomes of surgery operation, the mecha-
     nisms of the healthcare product/service life after utilization in the time.

    Products and services of healthcare are very dependent to the form of their delivery. As we see much
digitalization in healthcare channels and delivery systems, we can predict the much challenged hybrid
and semi digitalized services in healthcare what will be soon or coming years already on the market.
The products and services are also linked to the actual topic of empowered and digitally engaged patient,
what was discussed earlier. The healthcare services will be platform based what can also encourage the
direct to customer type of delivery (Maier et al., 2021). The service and product areas of healthcare will
be drivers of technical development. In this new environment tech companies can help healthcare provid-
ers to create the product and services in two ways: a) doing healthcare business with health providers to
create digital healthcare products and services, b) establish new platforms were healthcare product and
services can be created and delivered.
    The Virtual treatment and virtual clinical trial, monitoring patients at home is already very interesting
for marketing. Especially if we use the marketing knowledge and divide healthcare product or service
into levels, we will find that in many cases the core product, either fully or in some partial way can be
done in remotely working digital channels, implementing patients diagnostic or patient trial via moni-
toring approaches (Maier et al., 2021). For product development important is the big data, if Artificial
Intellect and Computerized approaches collect data, this makes patient care easier and more efficient.




                                                                                                           9
                                                                                                          ﻿
                                                                                      Healthcare Marketing




6.2. Place And the Channels of Distribution

Most revolutionary processes going not only into the new healthcare product/service development
where innovations and disruptive marketing has great stake, but into healthcare delivery (Salvini et
al., 2022). According long tradition healthcare product or service domestically was delivered in health
care providing organization, this was classical understanding of healthcare distribution during a lot of
centuries, but all changed when world started its digital migration (Senbekov et al.,2020). The health
2.0. will be on innovative development patch. Stopping people to visit hospitals and empowering them
by accredited and high quality information to participate in self-healthcare using approved ways of new
healthcare (Thimbleby, 2013).
    All digital systems providing patients with healthcare products and services, sometimes entitled as
smart product-service systems (SSP) integrate intelligent connected products and digital services into
solution bringing smart health care, what desires strong marketing understanding (Negash & Sarmiento,
2023). The healthcare services need the guideline of sustainable marketing, what emphasizing the
importance of behavior change from ordinary to healthy, the green marketing and consumer centrism
(Djakeli, 2023). In science literature the provision of remotely delivered clinical services via electronic,
audio and visual tools, is entitled as Telemedicine. It can be delivered into two forms: a) real time con-
sultation and real time care entitled as synchronous model and b) store and the forward approach entitled
as asynchronous model. The development of telemedicine gives doctors chances to develop some new
services, alike tele mentoring and even tele surgery. Among channels were health products and services
can be created are various channels combined under umbrella – digital health, which contains: e-health,
mobile health (m-health), telehealth or telemedicine. Digital health is the new industry and the new
market, where healthcare provision conceived and delivered to patients, via digital channels, with the
empowering of patients in the management of their health. This is something very new. In digital health,
the healthcare product and service is built by empowered patients, who is guided by healthcare providers
via digital healthcare channels.

6.3. Promotion And IMC Concept

For healthcare marketing different type of promotion activities are in relevance. The importance of
Advertising, Promotion, Sales Promotion, Direct Marketing and the Public Relation is very high for the
successful health care business. The healthcare Advertising can be divided into a) old media advertis-
ing for healthcare and b) new media advertising for healthcare. Moral, ethical and legal scenario use in
healthcare advertising suggests us that this theme must be regulated either by state or horizontal inde-
pendent systems of physician-patient associations for health and safety (Jalali & Pareek, 2010).
   The healthcare promotion - For better health Behavior change campaigns on social media are now
very effective. For example, tobacco counter marketing, almost change the behavior and reduced the
Tobacco use worldwide (Robinson et al., 2014).
   The healthcare sales promotion- is the instrument what involves, healthcare conferences, seminars,
publicity, exhibitions and special television, video blogs about innovations. If company is famous, the
sales promotion is easy because it already has earned media. The earned media what is used to pro-
mote the news of great hospitals, clinics, sanatoriums, doctors, medical associations is very interesting
medium connecting, those who are prospect buyers of healthcare and healthcare providers (Balogun,&
Ogunnaike, 2017).


10
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




    Direct marketing for healthcare is vital for success. All times the direct marketing plays great role
in new healthcare because it is based on data. The data in new healthcare analyze is the key to open the
new markets, to solve old problems by new solutions and direct offerings.
    Public Relation for healthcare all times urgent instrument, works for healthcare providers and pa-
tients. Public relation must be devoting to improve the attitude of the people, first of all, to the national
health system of the country, to vital reforms and trending events, to public and private hospitals, to
associations of nurses and medical associations. The Public Relation is also anti-crisis policy of health
care market, it is vital as a behavioral change from worst to good, making prevention of the diseases as
a very relevant goal either for the society and for the healthcare provider.

6.3.1. Social Media Marketing

Important is the role of social media for healthcare promotion and education (Gupta et al., 2013). The
trust what is very relevant in healthcare industry must be communicated by social media networking and
the access to word of mouth information in different type of social media. The types of social media are
also very important. Kaplan and Haenlein (2009) found important six different types of social media:
a) collaborative, b) blogs and microblogs, c) content communities, d) social networking sites, e) virtual
game worlds, f) virtual social worlds (Kaplan & Haenlein, 2009). Healthcare organizations with help
of social media create user generated content on the internet based applications, having some, practical,
useful, even ideological and marketing meaning for them and their customers. Because in 2023, as it
is estimated, already 4.9 billion people use social media, this is greatest talking and mind exchanging
space for healthcare providers and their patients either to work for image building or learn to have best
possible choice. Healthcare providers using social media promptly achieve good results. For example,
Mayo Clinic, make patients to tell their own stories about the medical treatment and their happiness to
be healthy after that. These videos on different type of social media channels attract other patients and
make them to ask questions directly to the organization. That makes the process to be customer centered
through social media marketing. The power of word of mouth communication is very valuable, and the
Mayo clinic encourages the Blogs where patients write their daily experience about their diseases and the
treatment methods. Through the podcasts special healthcare education is offered (Kaplan & Haenlein,
2012). The case of the attraction of customers by the social media can be the following:

•    The collaborative social media: coordinates the data management and the data flow among clin-
     ics, hospitals, physicians and nurses. Such collaborative sites are: OnPage, Symplr, BuzzMedical
     Messenger, TigerConnect Clinical Collaboration Platform, Vocera Platform and others. Very im-
     portant OnPage is working on search engine optimization opportunities to different clinics and
     healthcare organizations. As one happy customer wrote: “OnPage is the perfect product to be sure
     that critical alerts are never missed and it works perfectly with ConnectWise. You can create cus-
     tom schedules to alert on any service ticket you choose and setup escalations as well. This allows
     for an immediate response from your technicians” (OnPage, 2023). The system is built to increase
     the capacity for response teams, such as, those in healthcare and IT support. The number of such
     systems will only grow, what will make the remote healthcare progressive and comfortable.
•    Blogs and microblogs, developed by patients, prospect patients, doctors, hospitals are relevant
     medium, impacting customer behavior in healthcare market.




                                                                                                          11
                                                                                                        ﻿
                                                                                    Healthcare Marketing




•    Content communities: they provide patients with some information about the illnesses and the
     possible treatment (Johnson, et al., 2009). Patients connect with others with the same diagno-
     ses to exchange their opinion and start the curation. The online health communities became
     very popular during COVID -19. The examples for Online Health Communities are: Smart
     Patients, PatientsLikeMe, HealthBoards, MedHelp, DailyStrength, WebMD, GiveForward,
     HealthUnlocked, Inspire and others. For example, the Online Community entitled as Smart
     Patients, works as a search engine optimizer for cancer patients and cases, helping patients to find
     others with similar cases for conversation, experience sharing, suggestions and advises.
•    The social networking sites, the Health Platforms like Sermo, Doximity, DailyRounds, WeMedUp,
     Figure1, Student Doctors Network, Doctors Hangout and others do their job in best way. Let us
     discuss the advantage what they bring to the healthcare customers:

    for example, famous Sermo, is the most advanced website among healthcare providers is connecting
to verified and credentialed physicians from the globe. This site works in 150 countries and entitled as
virtual lounge of physicians. Here professionals ask questions to their peers anonymously. In 2023 the
number of members of Sermo is more than 800000. The Doximity - this collaborative social media site
units US based physicians where they interact to each other and the scientific articles, some innovative
works are discussed by members of this site, what makes many physicians to use this site regularly.
The half millions of doctors on this site stay innovative and more professional through information they
gather from their peers about many pivotal discoveries in healthcare technologies and human nature. The
DailyRounds is very interesting online community of Doctors, every physician who seeks expertise are
coming here for advise, some suggestion and knowledge based word. It is available on desktop, Android
and iOS for daily use. Very innovative WeMedUp is wonderful online community of physicians who
looks for information on healthcare job, healthcare related events and health technologies, innovations.
The Figure1 is the invaluable tool for saving the lives, collecting physicians working with rare disorders
from around the world, sharing anonymous X-ray photos about this rare diseases, making virtual online
meetings of doctors to find the solution. But the Student Doctors Network is offering to students the
conversations to experienced doctors, for idea exchanges and advises. This site already works from 1999
and encourages young medical staff. Is very interesting to analyze the role of DoctorsHangout where
every specialization of medical profession finds here some discussions among peers. The MomMD is
social site connecting page of female doctors and nurses. Very challenging is AmongDoctors, where
the experienced physicians can open their groups where doctors and nurses exchange their opinions and
suggestions. And finally AllNurses what is professional nurses virtual conference where all seek here
needed information and some advises, job opportunities, discussing salaries, employers, different cases
and give recommendations.
    Very challenging virtual worlds will be useful for healthcare, not only for promotion but for medi-
cal education, conferences, where something relevant must be shown, discussions and expert advises
(Ghanbarzadeh et al., 2014). In this area many relevant discoveries and innovations will be in next five
years, what will change the delivery model of healthcare (Liaw et al.,2018). Even this was predicted by
the first wave of internet technology development, especially in healthcare education (Johnson et al.,
2009). The staff meetings, especially the invitation of foreign located healthcare experts can be done
in normal way via virtual worlds (Taylor el al., 2020). Smartness of decision making and the united
mind of all members of healthcare team, is very vital in effectiveness of diagnoses of patients. That’s
why the ability to gather decision makers immediately, despite their geographical location, is relevant


12
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




for the saved lives and main ability of healthcare to cure patients, based on knowledge and experience
of best doctors and nurses. All these can be achieved by virtual gathering of medical academics and
doctors, via virtual world. The medical errors in healthcare frequently bring to death of patients and to
reduce the chances of errors, we can do many trainings. The medical trainings improving clinical skills
of medical staff can be well done in using the virtual worlds. Healthcare games can be also beneficial to
train the staff members (Bredl & Bösche, 2013). For example, the scenario based clinical simulations for
emergency health and also the communication learning via well-known Second Life Avatars is already
the practice of professional development for clinicians (Holloway, 2012). These avatars are very nice
vehicles for Health Behavior change modeling, therapeutic interventions learning and decision making.
    virtual social worlds – have great importance for healthcare dialog between the sides. In our time
when marketing is improved to the level of experiential marketing, the patient looks for something more
than only the service, patients seeks the persuasive dialogue what must be developed between health
organization and the customer. This dialogue now more comfortably must be managed via virtual world,
what has also social character (Williamson, 2015). The health-tracking technologies, personal analyt-
ics and the bio pedagogies of digitized health for society, plays big role in customer satisfaction. The
Metaverse will revolutionize the delivery of healthcare too. In this way the Chinese Ophthalmologists
already utilize the digital systems for virtual consultations and as they reported more than 10,000 eye-
related virtual visits were mediated by an AI chat bot (Tan et al., 2022).

6.4. Pricing

Healthcare service pricing positively related either to patient’s information search and review quality
(Wu et al., 2020). Mostly digital healthcare can reduce cost of the treatment, that means that hospitals
and clinics, managed care organizations and doctors, implementing some digital approaches can use cost
leadership strategy model and be competitive (Khanal, et al., 2016). From another side the affordability
of healthcare services is growing via their digitalization (Moro & Morea, 2020). The distinctiveness
of healthcare marketing is that there are healthcare products and services delivered but not with some
money equivalence (Purcarea, 2019). That means that importance of healthy customers is not only busi-
ness, it is great happiness of the whole country. The COVID-19 inspired countries to price the digitally
provided services. The term “Telehealth” refers to delivery of healthcare, health education and health
information services remotely. The Federal Medicare Program in the USA for example included telehealth
services like a psychotherapy, office visits, what was expanded to other services as well. Traditionally the
federal Medicare Program is using fee for service reimbursement. But the Digital Health Solutions still
don’t have strict coverage rules and guidelines. That’s why the reimbursement tactics is using bundled
payments. In difference to fee for service, bundled payment means the single process for all type of
treatments. To decrease the hospitalization, stay days, such tactics looks effective for American Health
Institutions (Gerke, 2020).

6.5. Physical Evidence

In healthcare marketing as a physical evidence can be assumed the internal environment of healthcare
organization and its external look, design, color, smell, landscape, gardening around and some flora and
fauna in it. All things what patients take with, during the leaving the health organization can be assumed
by healthcare marketers as the part of physical evidence (Sheaff et al.,2020). Sometimes the physical


                                                                                                         13
                                                                                                         ﻿
                                                                                     Healthcare Marketing




evidence is not positive, for example the ugly surgery cut on the nice body of patient is symbol of bad
care, this will work against the healthcare organization forever. Accordingly, the physical evidence of
healthcare must be managed well (Sreenivas et al, 2013).

6.6. People

The concept of the marketing mix and especially its 6th instrument entitled as a people has wonderful
meaning that, healthcare and the health, is not only responsible to direct patients but to whole people in
its environment, because the healthcare is very relevant for the success of the country and its citizens,
coming tourists, economy, social life and the future. If marketers entirely understanding the meaning of
this button, they will do their best to attract the hearts of people by a lot of type of marketing communi-
cations discussed by us above. First of all, the marketing vision to people based on the social marketing
concepts are effective. Without using the Social Marketing concepts in healthcare, marketers will not be
able to work effectively with people. Not only patients but the whole people must be the target of many
different type of socially directed communications of healthcare organization. The social marketing has
mission to change the wrong behavior of people and the person to the good. Frequently the same mis-
sion has the preventive medicine and its tries to change the behavior of prospect patients to make them
to avoid the illness. With the same sound mission and the goals, the healthcare organization can plan its
long-term influence on population through different type of marketing methodologies and instruments
to educate people, to attract them to know more about their health.

6.7. Process

It is important to communicate the healthcare manipulations to final customers with Value maintenance
and Superior Value, with persuasive communication and brand building process, to increase the image
of the process and establish high reputation on the market. Accordingly, the process and effective perfor-
mance of medical organization, based on the standards of superior quality, if it is allowed by ethical and
moral assumptions can be transparent, especially in Digital Era, where telemedicine and mobile health,
with some special healthcare technologies are expressing the union of best intellectual and experienced
human and AI sources, for final health of the patient. About the process developed by healthcare mar-
keters we speak in next paragraph.


7. THE PROCESS OF HEALTHCARE MARKETING

The process of healthcare marketing either is the satisfaction of needs, wants and demand of customers
or establishing new needs, wants and demand through innovative offerings via new medical solutions.
The changes in healthcare marketing we can take into account. In the physical healthcare market period,
healthcare marketing was trying to satisfy the customer. In first period of healthcare market digitali-
zation, the satisfaction of remote customer could be a target, but in the period of full digitalization of
healthcare market and use the modern healthcare marketing concept by clinics, the goal of the healthcare
company is the creation of empowered customer, or empowered patient, who will be much different than
simple customer of healthcare in the period of physical healthcare market (Robinson, 2013). But some




14
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




researchers think the enabling digital health companionship can be better than empowerment (Morley
& Floridi, 2019).
   The process of Healthcare Marketing in Digital Era is already different. We can divide the healthcare
marketing into traditional and digital. The traditional process includes:

•    Understanding the marketplace, patient’s needs, wants desires, their expectations. All these com-
     bined into patient’s expectation are very relevant to study. What influencing expectations? Personal
     expectations are driven by needs, values, roles, experience, information, intentions, attributions,
     interests, moods and emotions, perceived consequences of customers. But there are also social
     expectations of customers, for example socio demography, social norms, group pressures, tasks,
     conditions, restrictions, equity and the economic indicators of wealth (Thompson & Sunol, 1995).
     The healthcare is individual. There are no same health diagnoses. All patients are different and as
     healthcare marketing professionalizes it achieves more customer orientations. The digitalization
     gives new methods and methodologies to health organizations to be fully customer focused (Ford
     & Fottler, 2000).
•    To design the customer/Patient driven marketing strategy for healthcare. The customers of health-
     care are individuals as a patient, individuals as an non direct patients but those who want to pre-
     vent some diseases, also organizations, like insurers, employees, medical associations, patient as-
     sociations, professional unions, and public organizations like a Parliament office, state healthcare
     regulators, ministries and municipalities. Accordingly, the strategy of healthcare must feet patients
     as B2C and B2B customers. The paradox is that not all customers are the same “desirable” for
     the clinics. If we look from business perspective and have either regulated or limited strict prices
     looking like a capitation – as light health diagnosis patient has as more desirable this patient is.
     To avoid all these externalities in healthcare we need customer driven marketing strategy, what is
     shaped according the mission, vision and goals of healthcare organization (Wu et al., 2020). The
     healthcare moving from mass marketing to personalization, from low-tech to high-tech points and
     this must be the trends in future, backing by cost leadership, differentiation and cost focus strate-
     gies what are common also in non-healthcare markets. The main role of strategies in healthcare
     can be the identification of Strategic Competitive Advantage, to build high reputation of the com-
     pany, to exceed the expectation of customers, be persuasive and build strong healthcare brands.
•    Construct the integrated offline and online, marketing program to deliver to patients the superior
     healthcare value.
•    Engage the customers build long lasting relationships and inspire customer delight.
•    Exchange values with customers and win mutually, supporting healthiness and wellbeing.

   Patients and all customers of Healthcare can find the rational healthcare without borders by some new
technologies and methods what are already here. If in the beginning of Health Tourism patients were
seeking the new opportunities abroad, relay on health tourism (Cortez, 2008), now they can find them by
health tech companies. The Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) process as the process what is going in
the clinic physically has already two main delivery channels of healthcare. Accordingly, Remote Patient
Monitoring (RPM) will soon make unprecedented number of countries to restructure their healthcare
and be ready for remotely healthcare challenges. The remote working challenges improve and increase
the flexibility of healthcare services delivered remotely (Gigauri & Djakeli,2021 A).




                                                                                                       15
                                                                                                         ﻿
                                                                                     Healthcare Marketing




    The Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) can be defined as a digital system, transmitting patient’s health
related data to health care provider (Hayes et al., 2023) to improve patient’s wellbeing and decrease the
days of hospitalization, physical contact to health organization. Several clinical trials and researches
show that the Remote Patient Monitoring (RPM) improve patient health outcomes. The Remote Patient
Monitoring needs right segmentation. Which segment of patients are more rational to be with remotely
delivery health channels is the topic of healthcare marketing (Mecklai et al., 2021). This is the empow-
ering of patients as well.
    Some countries already succeed to Health Digitalization. Germans started Digital Health Reforms
in the period of COVID – 19. They prepared special Digital Healthcare Act – “Digitale–Versorgung–
Gesetz - DVG”, what can be the example for many other countries, because Germany has multi payer
health care system, with many insurance companies and social funding agency (Gerke et al., 2020).


8. CUSTOMER BEHAVIOR

The main principle of patient centered healthcare is that the voice of final patient, who is the user of
healthcare products and services with other, B2B customers of healthcare, like insurance companies,
state, and pharmacy, must be heard well everywhere in company (Djakeli, 2018). But the healthcare is
regulated market and the state policy is very relevant to understand the customer behavior on this market.
In 1990s, European National Health Systems were transformed into market way (Sheaff et al.,2020).
The social insurance healthcare and high solidarity was born in Germany. Taxation based universal
coverage healthcare was established in England, France, Italy, Spain. But at the end of 20th century, the
US Healthcare mind leadership exploded the old world, when Yale Professors developed the Diagnos-
tic Related Group model challenged everybody. For example, in 1992 Italy introduced as a system of
remuneration of prospective hospitals, the model, very alike of marketing segmentation, entitled as the
Diagnosis Related Groups (DRG), with entire experience of American health (Nonis et al.,2018). Either
Bismarck or Beveridge models were exporting its experience, systems and findings to other countries
the behavior of customers became dependent from those (Nuti et al., 2012).
    The customer behavior in Healthcare is more linked to healthcare financing and delivery systems
established by different countries. For example, the implementation of Diagnostic Related Groups in
Italy, decreased Hospital admission rate of patients, especially among Diabetics, but increased the se-
verity of illness. So called “DRG Creep” phenomena, shows terrible moral hazard when patients seem
sicker and the system has high ethical problems (Lous,1999).
    The hypothesis, that patients and prevention healthcare customers tend to be more consumptive in
the era of digitalization is worthy for great marketing research, but also without it any observation of
customer behavior, watching digital healthcare platform about current or preventive medicine, gives us
more conformation to this hypothesis. According the strengthening the digital healthcare the consump-
tiveness of consumers will only grow (Fahmi et al., 2022).
    If customers of ordinary healthcare had been developing the self-leadership for patients to well or-
ganize their healthy skills and habits, through digital health we face new marketing leadership, entitled
as IT based leadership, what can combine, e-health, telemedicine, artificial intelligence technologies,
virtual monitoring and trial systems, mobile health, cloud computing and others.
    What is the customer empowering process in healthcare market? The adoption process by digitalized
healthcare customers will be the following: the patients willing to know more about the benefits of digital


16
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




health, they learn and healthcare providers educate them. The patients have maturity stage in empower-
ing and they start to use the digital healthcare fully, these patients will be the innovators. According the
Rogers Diffusion model innovators will be followed by early adopters of Remotely Health. The other
cluster who will follow the early adopters are three groups of healthcare customers: early majority, late
majority and laggards (Ward, 2013). To satisfy the needs wants and desires of customers’ healthcare
institutions now use an Artificial Intelligence and healthcare marketing must be very attentive to the
skills of its properly use. The AI can be used well in biomedicine, analyzing the healthcare diagnosis,
optimization of healthcare treatment for the patient, turn healthcare process into value based healthcare.
Important is the role of AI in Medical Educational process.
    The process of healthcare is long period driven by different type of quality management standards
and the healthcare management rules (Gigauri, & Djakeli, 2021 C). The Empowering Patient concept,
what is now much relevant vehicle towards remotely delivered healthcare, is also vital part of the process.
The patient empowerment concept can guarantee an effective self-management to improve health and
well-being of patients, after their education in self-health-care what answers the theoretical and practical
requirements of modern healthcare marketing (Ben Ayed & El Aoud, 2017).


9. FINDINGS AND RECOMMENDATIONS

The discussion about health and healthcare uniqueness is well known. Itself healthcare as an industry
doing products and services can be much differentiated from any other industry for several reasons
(World Health Organization, 2021):
    Firstly, we see strong informational asymmetry in Healthcare Markets. What is seen and knowledge
able by doctor cannot easily be understood by patients.
    Secondly the healthcare is not only creating products and services according the needs wants and
desires of patients by this very innovative and challenging industry creates either the new technologies,
methodologies of healthcare, fresh new solutions of old problems with the healthcare products and
services, what inspire new needs, wants and demands from the patients. Even it can be the creation of
new customers through development of new demand.
    Thirdly, the healthcare is frequently involved into life and death matters of the customers and its
products and services proved by happiness and the life status of their patients, measured by different
indicators. These indicators can be grouped as Quality Adjusted Life years(QUALY), some social and
behavioral indicators in Electronic Health Records(EHR), the 29 indicators, divided into five subscales
(Lavinia & Elena, 2015). There are also health risks and behavior related eight additional indicators:
healthcare immunization, vaccination, insured health and personal care needs, personal care provid-
ing supply, blood tests and physical exams and more socially oriented indicators about self-related
health status, air quality index, high school diploma, health literacy rate, employment status and others
(Sokoya,2022). All these indicators healthcare marketer must use in the process of healthcare market
segmentation, targeting, differentiation and positioning.
    Fourthly patients, the direct customers of healthcare, either cannot decide themselves from which
health provider to choose the healthcare service, or patients are totally unable to manage their needs,
wants and healthcare desires, because they are in the hands of referrals. The referral relationship is
common on health. Sometimes called as refugees of healthcare systems, referral relationship is under a
fierce critic (Enumah, 2022).


                                                                                                         17
                                                                                                          ﻿
                                                                                      Healthcare Marketing




    The clinics and hospitals need to realize their health strategies according their mission and choose
either cost leadership, differentiation or cost focus strategies. The niche health companies try to develop
cost focus strategies emphasizing organizations having minimal costs where they differentiate much and
are experts of the niche (Djakeli, 2018). Interesting is that some healthcare systems motivate the high
role of the state but others give only minimum role to the state interventions. Because the good health is
the happiness and bad health is a mortal, the quality of healthcare never can be sacrificed to some other
goals. If Patients in traditional physical healthcare delivery markets were less able to discuss the quality
of health, in fully digitalized healthcare, well engaged and empowered patients can easily understand
the determinants of healthcare service values they received (Kay, 2007).
    Modern healthcare marketing in digital age creating the phenomenon of new healthcare. During CO-
VID-19 pandemic developments, new wave of digitalization started in various industries and especially
in healthcare, where most interesting digital devices started to work to increase the social capital (Kim &
Lee, 2021). The social capital of the general publics is created by healthcare and the role of Marketing in
this process is high. The digital devices and digitalization with its abilities to increase connectedness of
people and healthcare organizations is working well for improving social capital (Hawe & Shiell, 2000).
There are different types of digital applications what can help to healthcare. The smart devices, block
chains, virtual clinical trials, telemedicine and 3D printers, organized by the Artificial Intelligence (AI),
machine learning (ML), with huge importance of informational clouds, the big data and the different
types of remotely delivered medical services, will be big climb of success for entire health.
    Something what looks like science fiction today will be routine tomorrow (Thimbleby, 2013). Imple-
mentation of digital technologies establish new possibilities in healthcare markets. The revolution of
information technologies, challenging the digital migration and creation of Artificial Intelligence made
Healthcare to be:

•    More accessible and affordable,
•    Flexible to access,
•    Available and comparable to other similar solutions,
•    Better as a medical treatment,
•    Smart for customers/patients to write their feedbacks about their satisfaction by healthcare
     organizations,
•    Better to inspire biomedical researches in internet,
•    Accurate to diagnostic and consulting,
•    Improving equality of urban and rural patients to give them better consultation and a equal
     treatment,
•    Empowering the patients in self – healthcare,
•    Popularize the prevention,
•    Receive the exceptional patient care with AI, telemedicine, for example the situation when the
     doctors could see inside the body of the patient and treat her or him precisely than ever before,
     even from remote.




18
﻿
Healthcare Marketing




10. CONCLUSION

The concept of healthcare marketing firstly appeared as a promoter of clinics, their services and happy
faces of healed ones, but high competition on the market of healthcare products and services, in the
period than this market turned as a market of buyers, demanded cleverer marketing. Because healthcare
industry mostly is inflation oriented, with high cost of the medical technologies and the treatments, it is
obvious that the stakeholders of big clinics and health hubs, were thinking to use much strategic planning,
knowledge of management and smart methods of financing to try to save the markets and the purchasing
ability of healthcare service customers, finally fully adopted the marketing concept.
    In the end of 90s the old and traditional concept of advertising was senseless, because of high prices
of broadcasting television giant companies in America. This was inspiratory of the Integrated Marketing
Communication Concepts, complaining the different type of media into one great persuasive marketing
instrument. The healthcare companies started to use the social media channels but soon the fourth indus-
trial revolution, creating more chances for healthcare providers to be integrated with Internet of Things
(IoT), cloud computing, Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, big data and analytics, completely
challenged the healthcare market. This revolutionizing type of challenge, entitled as digital migration,
affected the healthcare, establishing the telemedicine, mobile medicine, channels to deliver not only the
information but the healthcare product to final user. So the new Healthcare 4.0. henceforth known as
“HC4.0” was created.
    Because the healthcare marketing is very comprehensive in contenting institutions and meaning and
it is assumed as the process organizer for entire healthcare service industry, with financiers, insurers,
healthcare policy makers, health providers and controllers, it is becoming the scene for the new ways
to create a customer. With AI it can change the design and nature of healthcare forever. The Artificial
Intelligence, Virtual Reality and fast digitalization, changing the healthcare Marketing from managing
concept to creation one. The medicine with its great minds, innovations, genial solutions, data based
inventions and discoveries, with the concept of modern marketing is knocking the doors of any current
or prospective patient, offering them comfort and solution.
    The modern healthcare marketing goals started from patient engagement, education, patient empow-
erment and the new self-health-care able customer creation, increasing the role of healthcare platforms,
their promotion, branding and patient satisfaction, is finalized by the intellectual power of empowered
patient as a co-creator of its own health.
