                                                        Chapter 2
               The Role of Social Media
               in Healthcare Marketing
                                                      Nugzar Todua
                                           https://orcid.org/0000-0002-1486-2141
                                   Ivane Javakhishvili Tbilisi State University, Georgia

                                                     Charita Jashi
                                   Ivane Javakhishvili Tbilisi State University, Georgia

                                                       Nia Todua
                                   Ivane Javakhishvili Tbilisi State University, Georgia


ABSTRACT
The chapter discusses the features of using social media in the healthcare industry. It is emphasized that
the role of social media in the era of digital technologies is especially increasing in the market of medical
services, which leads to greater involvement of consumers in the field of healthcare. Digital technolo-
gies are gradually being introduced in the field of healthcare in Georgia, and an increasing number of
medical organizations are using social media marketing to communicate with patients. Nevertheless, the
use of social media by healthcare organizations is insufficient. One of the reasons for this is the lack of
research in this field. With this in mind, this chapter presents a study that considers the impact of social
media marketing activities on healthcare consumer behavior. Social media marketing activities, consumer
engagement, consumer trust, and behavioral intention are selected as the main variables of the research.
A conceptual model of the research has been developed, and relevant hypotheses have been formulated.



1. INTRODUCTION

Social media is an integral part of modern society and is gradually becoming a significant marketing
tool that provides companies with ample opportunities to interact with their consumers (Abid et al.,
2023). Social media has become a perfect platform for companies to popularize and sell their product,
find new information, and useful products, and communicate with their friends (Jacobson et al., 2020).

DOI: 10.4018/979-8-3693-0679-6.ch002



       Copyright © 2024, IGI Global. Copying or distributing in print or electronic forms without written permission of IGI Global is prohibited.
﻿
The Role of Social Media in Healthcare Marketing




Social media has become a valuable tool for healthcare knowledge management (Khan et al., 2021). New
technologies and ways of treatment as well as the growth of expectations in society that they are going
to have access to high-quality healthcare create the need for new efficient strategies that will lower the
cost of treatment (Kulkov et al., 2021; Çağlıyor et al., 2022). Social media in the public health agenda is
another player in the marketing world that inspires and influences potential customers with the usage of
social media platforms (Kostigina et al., 2020; Carlsson-Szlezak et al., 2020). Healthcare professionals
use social media to build professional relationships with their colleagues and to share information (Rolls
et al., 2016). In the era of digital communication, healthcare providers recognize that social media with
its diverse programs is the best tool for public health communications (Rolls et al., 2016). Social media
is the most effective way of sharing new information in the healthcare sphere as it can reach the widest
and most diverse audience (Swan et al., 2019).
    Healthcare providers use social media to promote their organizations, also to establish strong re-
lationships with existing and potential customers, and raise awareness of their own brand (Mondal et
al., 2022). As for patients, they receive information about their health condition through social media
(Kamarudin et al., 2022; Freire et al., 2023).
    Nowadays, healthcare organizations face many challenges. First of all, consumers move from a pas-
sive state to an active participant in the process of providing medical services (Crie & Chebat, 2013;
Osei-Frimpong, 2017; Hung et al., 2023). They are increasingly using digital applications and various
technologies that help them to be directly involved in protecting their well-being. Accordingly, healthcare
organizations try not to stay behind and improve the methods of providing medical services, in which
marketing approaches play a special role. Scholars emphasize the fact that one of the tools for success
in the field of healthcare is marketing skills (Anderson et al., 2018; Chaves et al., 2021). That is why
healthcare providers increasingly use patient-oriented approaches (Crie & Chebat, 2013; Pilon et al., 2023).
    Practice shows that social media is an essential part of the marketing and communication strategies
of many medical organizations. The use of social media gives health systems a lot of opportunities.
It helps healthcare organizations and physicians build relationships with patients, Openly share their
opinions and get feedback from them, strengthen customer confidence, and enhance the attractiveness
of their brands. Through online communication, patients, hospitals, and physicians form the foundation
of positive relationships. Also, audience-targeting advertising through social media platforms helps
healthcare organizations work individually with patients. Users are actively utilizing the Internet and
social networks to seek health information (Zhou et al., 2018). It is also notable that 60% of social media
users, compared to other groups, trust doctors’ posts more (Chen et al., 2018). We should also note the
fact that today’s doctors, due to their busy work schedules, have less time to post relevant posts on social
media. That is why marketers help doctors regularly post messages with original and quality content on
their social networks. As we can see, social media, when used wisely, can be a supportive tool for the
development of the health care system as well as for the care of patients by raising their awareness and
promoting health programs. Social media can make a significant contribution to improving the quality
of medical services; however, we must also consider the risks associated with the protection of personal
data and the accuracy of information (Alshakhs & Alanzi, 2018; Liu et al., 2021).
    Social media changes the nature and speed of communication between healthcare organizations and
physicians. Today, a significant number of doctors and patients use social media to raise knowledge
about healthcare and disease. In turn, the use of social media is quite effective for healthcare promotion
and behavior change (Korda & Itani, 2013). Hospitals and physicians actively involved in social media
can identify and solve patients’ problems in a timely manner, leading to increased customer satisfaction


                                                                                                         27
                                                                                                          ﻿
                                                          The Role of Social Media in Healthcare Marketing




and loyalty (Jackson et al., 2021). The introduction of innovations in the Internet space contributes to the
development of digital health technologies. Under the influence of social media marketing, consumer
access to digital healthcare tools is expanding (Swan et al., 2019).
    Websites can help patients get more involved with treatment phases by educating and helping them
consistently manage their disease. Social media utilization in knowledge sharing can be considered a
vitally important issue not only on a personal level but also for organizations. Some hospitals even have
websites and apps where people can get free consultations from doctors or even book an online treatment
procedure (Koumpouros et al., 2015). Many researchers pay attention to social media and Knowledge
Sharing because they play a huge role in decision-making regarding health services. Knowledge Shar-
ing through social media is beneficial for all stakeholders in healthcare to identify existing challenges
and find out how to resolve them. Good communication and knowledge sharing renew trust and good
relationships, which are particularly important between participants with the same healthcare roles and
interests (Aghdam et al., 2021). Providing health knowledge management, particularly for doctors and
patients, is significant for increasing their awareness, obtaining new technologies abilities, and skills,
and empowering medical infrastructure to guarantee effective healthcare policy (Ghalavand et al., 2020).
Such an approach was especially important in the context of the COVID-19 pandemic (Renu, 2021;
Prasetya et al., 2023).
    Digital technologies are being used by a number of Georgian medical organizations to advertise
their services and foster better client interactions. Patients and the general public in Georgia should be
willing to give healthcare institutions that use social media marketing their trust and gratitude. This
was particularly noticeable during the COVID-19 outbreak. Georgia’s government has implemented a
unified electronic healthcare system, and it has worked very hard to adjust the healthcare system and
bring in cutting-edge medical technologies in response to the continuous changes in the world economy
(Healthcare and Social Issues Committee of the Parliament of Georgia, 2017). However, these actions
are insufficient. Regretfully, Georgian digital marketing in the medical field is still in its infancy. We
may conclude that in the Georgian healthcare sector, using social media marketing techniques is still
seen as innovative. As a result, in the Georgian medical services sector, customer awareness and sat-
isfaction with demands are often poor (Todua & Jashi, 2018). The majority of Georgian customers do
not get timely or sufficient information on the latest advancements in the healthcare industry. This is
due, in part, to the fact that less research has been done on the effects of social media marketing on
local customers’ behavior in the healthcare industry in Georgian reality (Mladenović et.al.,2023). The
following, in our opinion, is the most crucial of these issues: The impact of Social Media Marketing
activities provided by healthcare organizations on Georgian consumer’s engagement, and trust, as well
as the impact of engagement and trust on purchase intention. All of this necessitates precise scientific
investigation. The course of pertinent research was conditioned by the significance of the problem, the
issues in this field, and the need for their scientific investigation. With this in mind, the study’s goal is
to determine how social media marketing activities impact consumer behavior in the healthcare sector.
The following Research Questions (RQ) are presented in light of the foregoing:

RQ 1: How Social Media Marketing activities conducted by health care organizations impact on Georgian
    consumers engagement in social media?
RQ 2: How Social Media Marketing activities conducted by health care organizations impact on Georgian
    consumers trust in social media?




28
﻿
The Role of Social Media in Healthcare Marketing




RQ 3: How Georgian consumers engagement in social media impact on their behavioral intention re-
    garding healthcare?
RQ 4: How Georgian consumers trust in social media impact on their behavioral intention regarding
    healthcare?


2. LITERATURE REVIEW AND DEVELOPMENT OF RESEARCH HYPOTHESES

These days, the organization’s most valuable intangible asset is seen to be the creation of a customer
relations vision (Pansari & Kumar, 2017). According to recent studies, client participation on social
media makes it easier for businesses to co-create market characteristics for new products and corporate
values (Hollebeek et al., 2019). According to research, client interaction enhances social identification,
good attitudes, emotional benefits, and cognitive functions. Accordingly, a certain mental state that
consumers develop throughout their interactions with brands and companies is referred to as customer
engagement (Hollebeek et al., 2014).
    In the healthcare industry, social media consumer interaction is very crucial. For instance, client
communication is facilitated via hospital-sponsored activities including online forums, health postings,
and preventative webinars. As a result, these initiatives guarantee that patrons adopt a positive outlook
and enhance their overall experience (Kumar & Pansari, 2016). Social media platforms are transform-
ing consumer interaction in a number of ways. First, social media platforms have the power to incite
client participation by compelling businesses to examine customer interaction data in order to provide
better customer service (Carlson et al., 2018). Conversely, social media makes it feasible to get excellent
levels of client interaction. Users of social media platforms can establish connections with a wide range
of people and organizations (Schivinski et al., 2016). Third, location-based services are now available
on social media platforms to assist draw in new users and cultivate relationships with them (Wang &
Stefanone, 2013).
    Engaging with clients on social media is essential in the healthcare sector. For example, hospital-
sponsored initiatives like health posters, preventative webinars, and online chats make it easier to interact
with patients. Thus, these programs ensure that customers have a favorable attitude and improve their
experience (Kumar & Pansari, 2016). Research shows that patient engagement in the online activities of
medical organizations has a significant impact on the effectiveness of their healthcare (Mirzaei & Esmaeil-
zadeh, 2021). The literature review mentioned above provides support for the following hypothesis (H):

H1: Social Media marketing activities conducted by health care organizations has a positive impact on
     consumers engagement in social media.

    When people are engaged in online activities, the trust issue becomes even more crucial (Kwortnik
Jr & Han, 2011). The marketing literature defines “trust” in a variety of ways, but honesty, capacity,
competence, and recognition are the foundations for each definition (Pirson et al., 2017). The internet
world is characterized by danger and unpredictability. Reputation is built through persuasion-based trust.
Benevolence relates to the relationship between the seller and the buyer, while it involves the belief that
the other party to the transaction merits confidence (Chiu et al., 2012). Online community perceptions
of trust are connected to online behavior. In the context of an online community, trust provides a means
for each member to cultivate personal connections with others and encourage them to draw others into


                                                                                                          29
                                                                                                         ﻿
                                                         The Role of Social Media in Healthcare Marketing




the network. Thus, confidence plays a crucial role in shaping customers’ intentions as well as their pur-
chase behavior (Asti et al., 2021). It is noteworthy that the online activities of healthcare organizations
are an important factor that encourages patients to seek high-tech medical services (Ahmed et al., 2023).
Drawing on the aforementioned literature assessment, the subsequent hypothesis is posited:

H2: Social media marketing activities conducted by health care organizations has a positive impact on
     consumers trust in social media.

    Examining the intention to buy is a crucial aspect of researching customer behavior on the internet.
One of the main features of consumer behavior is purchase intention, which is connected to it (Oosthuizen
et al., 2015). Businesses must exercise caution when evaluating purchase intentions since anticipating
customer behavior is crucial to their success (Baabdullah et al., 2019). Simultaneously, it should be
mentioned that customers’ attractiveness and/or loyalty to items is mostly determined by their positive
purchase intentions (Hong & Cho, 2011). According to Farah (2017) and Kahraman & Kazançoğlu (2019),
intention indicates the subjective likelihood that customers would purchase the goods and carry out a
particular activity. Based on the aforementioned, we may ascertain which products consumers should
buy and when they will go shopping by using their purchase intention (Hajli et al., 2017).
    Social media, which allows for a two-way dialogue between the company and its clients, has proved
ideal for the development of buying intentions. A connection like this strengthens a favorable opinion
of the brand and influences purchase intention favorably (Hutter et al., 2013). Furthermore, research has
shown that social media marketing initiatives have an impact on consumers’ intentions to make purchases
(Yadav & Rahman, 2017). Consumer engagement in social media plays an important role in shaping their
healthcare awareness and behavioral intention (Khan et al., 2021). Many studies show that trust influences
patients’ intention to use the technologies offered by healthcare organizations (Zulman et al., 2011; Liu
et al., 2023). In light of the literature review mentioned above, the following Hypotheses are presented:

H3: Consumers engagement in social media has a positive impact on their behavioral intention regard-
    ing healthcare;
H4: Consumers trust in social media has a positive impact on their behavioral intention regarding
    healthcare.


3. RESEARCH METHODOLOGY

In order to generalizations about ideas, we employed the deductive approach (Bryman & Bell, 2011). The
ideas were then examined by testing the hypotheses (Bryman & Bell, 2011; Malhotra, 2010; Saunders et
al., 2019). The following were covered in our study design: determining the information required; choos-
ing research methods; creating a questionnaire and identifying its measuring characteristics; calculating
sample size; and data analysis. The study’s design required gathering both primary and secondary data.
We collected secondary data from public sources, including statistics reports from Georgian govern-
ment agencies. In this study, primary data are being collected using the quantitative research approach,
which offers the use of unified data collection tools (Saunders et al., 2019). In response, we conducted
a consumer survey using a specially created questionnaire and descriptive methods.




30
﻿
The Role of Social Media in Healthcare Marketing




    The variables in this study are developed using quantitative and deductive methods. Our objective is
to assess the regression and correlation relationships between the independent variables (social media
marketing activities) and the dependent variables (social media engagement, trust, and purchase inten-
tion). We can create a conceptual research model that graphically illustrates the influence of social media
marketing activities by healthcare organizations on customer behavior based on the above-described
structure and literature review (Figure 1).
    The primary means of data collection in our study was an internet questionnaire. Furthermore, a
self-administered survey was used in this investigation. Because of this strategy, the self-administration
technique of directly gathering data from respondents is easy, quick, and affordable (Saunders et al.,
2019). The questionnaire’s questions were derived from the authors’ viewpoints as stated in this paper’s
literature review section. Based on a deductive methodology, the variables and measuring items in this
study were developed using pertinent literature (see table 1). Multiple-choice questions are utilized
to provide the responses, and a 5-point Likert scale is employed (1=strongly disagree, 2=disagree,
3=neutral, 4=agree, and 5=strongly agree). The questionnaire took about fifteen to twenty minutes to
complete because to its design.
    We studied the general population using the probability sampling approach. Georgia was the research
area, and it was chosen in a way that made it representative. To get the sample size, we applied a formula
from statistics (Krejcie & Morgan, 1970):

           x 2N P (1 − P )
S=                                 ,                                                                 (1)
     d 2 (N − 1) + x 2P (1 − P )


    Where: S = required sample size; x2= the table value of chi-square for 1 degree of freedom (df) at
the desired confidence level; N = the population size; P = the population proportion (assumed to be .50
since this would provide the maximum sample size); d = the degree of accuracy expressed as a propor-
tion (.05). Value of chi-square for 95% confidence level is 3,8416 (1,96 X 1,96).

   Georgia’s population as of the 2023 census is 3726400, of which 229400 are Internet users over the
age of 18, which is the population our study is aimed at (National Statistics Office of Georgia, 2023).
Using formula (1) and the aforementioned data, the sampling size will be:

              1, 962 x 2294000 x 0, 5 x (1 − 0, 5)
S=                                                                 = 373, 2
       0, 052 (2294000 − 1) + 1, 962 x 0, 5 (1 − 0, 5)


   Therefore, 374 persons were chosen as the study’s sample size. Through email and social media,
we reached out to 572 responders; of them, 425 responded. The statistical program SPSS 27 was used
to process the data. We employed the Pearson’s Correlation Analysis, Multiple Regression Analysis
and Dispersion Analysis, as well as Linear Regression Model among the statistical analytic techniques.
   Researchers use the Cronbach’s alpha coefficient to determine reliability measures. If the reliability
coefficient is 0.60 or more, it is deemed satisfactory (Nunnally, 1967). Table 2 provides the reliability
data that we were obtained by us. Table 4 demonstrates that for all six variables, the Cronbach’s alpha




32
﻿
The Role of Social Media in Healthcare Marketing




coefficient was 0.945, and for each variable, it was greater than 0.8, indicating a much higher reliable
coefficient.


4. MAIN FINDINGS AND DISCUSSION

The sample of respondents were uncovered by our marketing study, which was categorized by several
criteria. Table 3 displays the sampling characteristics based on the number of respondents and the per-
centage. According to the poll, the majority of participants (47.1%) are between the ages of 18 and 24,
with 1.6% being 65 or older. Women make up 63.5% of the responders, while males make up 36.5%.
    Next, we explored whether there is a connection between social media marketing activities and
customer engagement. As it turned out, such a connection is statistically reliable (P <0.001), and the
correlation coefficient is positive (r = 0.267) (Table 4). As mentioned above, the first hypothesis re-
lates to the impact of social media marketing activities on consumers engagement. Regression analysis
is used to test the H1 hypothesis. Table 4 shows that the model we developed is reliable. (P<0.001);

F=32.581>Fcritical=3.84. The H1 hypothesis is supported. Consequently, social media marketing activi-
ties have a positive impact on consumers engagement. In addition, R square =0.072, which allows us
to say that 7.2% of the activities are caused by engagement, which is quietly low. Accordingly, the rest
is due to other factors.
     In the next step, we explored whether there is a connection between social media marketing activities
and Trust. As it turned out, the result of the study is statistically acceptable (P <0.001), and the correla-
tion coefficient is positive (r = 0.389) (Table 5). As mentioned above, the third hypothesis relates to the
impact of social media activities on Trust. With this in mind, we used regression analysis to test the H2
hypothesis. As can be seen from Table 5, the model we developed is reliable (P <0.001; F = 75.490>
Fcritical = 3.84). The H2 hypothesis is supported. Therefore, social media activities have a positive impact
on Trust. Also, R square = 0.151, which allows us to say that 15.1% of the social media marketing activ-
ity is caused by Trust, while the rest is due to other factors.
     To explore of the impact of consumers engagement and Trust on Behavioral Intention in social
media, we first examined whether there is a connection between consumer engagement in social media
marketing activities and behavioral intention. As it turned out, such a relationship is statistically reli-
able (P <0.001), and the correlation coefficient is positive (r = 0.571) (Table 6). As noted above, the

5th hypotheses relate to consumers engagement’s impact on behavioral intension. We used regression
analysis to test the H4 hypothesis. As can be seen from Table 6, the model we developed is reliable (P
<0.001 F=204.83>Fcritical =3.84. The H3 hypothesis is supported. Consequently, consumers engagement
affects behavioral Intention. R square = 0.326, which allows us to say that 32.6% of Behavioral Intention
is caused by Engagement while the rest is due to other factors.
    As mentioned above, the 5th hypotheses reflect the impact of Trust on Behavioral Intention. With this
in mind, we used regression analysis to test the H4 hypothesis. As Table 7 shows the model we developed
is reliable (P<0.001; F=325.80>Fcritical =3.84). The H5 hypothesis is supported. Consequently, the Trust
affects Behavioral Intention. Also, R square = 0.435, which allows us to say that 43.5% of Behavioral
Intention is caused by the Trust while the rest is due to other factors.


5. CONCLUSION

This survey indicates that social media are now an important aspect of Georgian consumers’ lives, and
they are used often. Consumers in Georgia are increasingly turning to the Internet for medical information,

                                                           The Role of Social Media in Healthcare Marketing




and they are particularly interested in health-related content. According to our study, Georgian customers
are still acting in a somewhat passive manner. Furthermore, there is a notable lack of confidence among
Georgian customers about the material disseminated on social media. The regression study produced
statistically significant values that illustrate the relationships between the social media activities provided
by healthcare organizations and consumer engagement, trust, and behavioral intentions. Furthermore,
linear regression models that take into consideration the relationships between the aforementioned vari-
ables are obtained. By using these models, we may ascertain the extent to which a rise in social media
activities provided by healthcare organizations endeavors could influence customer conduct.


6. THEORETICAL AND PRACTICAL IMPLICATIONS

This study has several theoretical ramifications for how social media marketing is used in the health-
care sector. First of all, it’s critical to acknowledge that there are still glaring gaps in the field of social
media marketing, even if the topic is quite wide and has been the focus of much research. This indicates
that fewer studies have been conducted to determine a relationship between the social media marketing
strategies employed by healthcare organizations and factors that influence consumer behavior, such as
customer engagement, trust, and behavioral intention. This work contributes to the understanding of the
link between these factors. A wealth of information from this study may be used in future research to
gain a deeper comprehension of how social media marketing affects consumer behavior.
    The following are the main contributions made by this study from a managerial perspective: First,
the study’s conclusions will help managers in the healthcare sector better understand how social media
marketing campaigns may be an effective means of establishing connections with both present and po-
tential clients. Second, healthcare organizations may use the study findings to guide the creation of their
business plans. Third, health care providers will be able to develop marketing strategies that will help
them accomplish their goals by having a deeper grasp of the purchasing perspectives of their clients.


7. RESEARCH LIMITATIONS AND DIRECTION FOR FUTURE RESEARCH

The study took into account certain limitations. First of all, the study’s required sample size of respond-
ers was constrained by pragmatic considerations. To be more precise, the study’s sample is not entirely
typical of the population, particularly when it comes to all age categories. The over-24 age group needs
to get special attention. The research was primarily focused on the 18–24 age bracket, which makes up
the bulk of Internet and social media users in Georgia even if it cannot be considered a whole popula-
tion. The poll was mostly based on a survey of Georgian consumers in Tbilisi, the capital of Georgia.
Since most Georgians reside in Tbilisi and are thus Internet users, the limitation was placed in place.
    Future studies into social media marketing activities and associated customer behavior in the Georgian
healthcare sector will build upon the findings of this study. This work presents a number of intriguing
avenues for further research. It first has to do with how social media marketing campaigns for healthcare
organizations affect consumers happiness and loyalty. The creation and execution of such marketing
initiatives are unaccustomed to Georgian reality, which offers fascinating research opportunities. Future
studies should examine the ways in which consumer loyalty and satisfaction with healthcare organiza-
tions social media marketing campaigns influence their buying decisions.
