ABSTRACT
Artificial intelligence (AI) technologies have made enormous strides recently and are already pervasive in
many aspects of our daily life. Numerous efforts are being undertaken in the healthcare industry to apply
AI technology for useful medical treatments. With the quick advancements in hardware performance and
machine learning algorithms, it is anticipated that AI technology would be crucial in efficiently process-
ing and utilizing vast amounts of health and medical data. However, the AI technology differs from the
current medical technology in a number of distinctive ways. There are still few medical professionals
and members of the general public that accept AI in healthcare, and there are several challenges about
the security and dependability of AI technology applications. This chapter describes the state of current
AI research and application in the healthcare industry and identifies the various challenges that need
to be addressed and suggest how to resolve them.


INTRODUCTION

When artificial intelligence (AI) first emerged in the United States in 1956, its main purpose is a task
of self-learning algorithm created by studying the data that was already available. Following years of
DOI: 10.4018/979-8-3693-0679-6.ch007



       Copyright © 2024, IGI Global. Copying or distributing in print or electronic forms without written permission of IGI Global is prohibited.
﻿
Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




development, AI has made tremendous development in applications such as medical image processing,
medical process optimization, medical education, and other domains. It has gradually become a compo-
nent of standard medical care (Jiang, 2021). Since AI technology has developed so quickly, a variety of
problems have arisen, making it less common in clinical and public health settings. Artificial intelligence
technology has advanced significantly recently and is now present in many facets of our daily lives.
    The health care sector is making several attempts to integrate Artificial Intelligence technology to
treatment patient. There are some notable ways in which AI technology is different from contemporary
medical technologies. There are a few gaps in the current system that must be filled in order for AI to
be employed in healthcare more regularly and successfully.
    Future advancements in healthcare technology are anticipated to result from ongoing improvements
in artificial intelligence technologies. As a result, lot of research work is done and researchers already
published a significant number of research papers with enormous volumes of health related data by ap-
plying various technologies for patient diagnosis and treatment.
    Furthermore, most of the researchers demonstrated using Artificial Intelligence in the healthcare
industry produces superior results to those obtained using current technologies. Encrypting medical
data, forecasting patient history of a disease using multiple sources, and analyzing medical photos with
Artificial Intelligence technology to categorize images and apply for therapies are some of the studies
being conducted in this area.
    In this chapter, we outline the present barriers preventing AI from becoming widely accepted as well
as the current use of Artificial Intelligence in medicine (Park, 2020). Chapter first section describes the
state of current AI research and application in the healthcare industry and analyzes the problems that
require fixing. We also discuss about potential approaches to tackling issues.


CURRENT STATE OF AI IN HEALTHCARE

This section describes the related articles published in a crisp manner.

AI Applications in the Healthcare Industry

These days artificial technology is making new advances since it affects additional psychological areas,
including judgment, intelligence and knowledge. World Health Organization (Ziglio, 2004) states that
60% of aspects relating to one’s health and quality of life are related to one’s habits, including exercise,
maintaining a healthy diet, reclining, reducing anxiety, using antidepressants, and socializing.
    Automation technology with AI is now able to offer lifestyle modifications including daily affirmations
based on the psychological signs of the individuals. Artificial Intelligence based methods are anticipated
to radically alter the functioning of medical systems, patient interactions and patient care services in order
to enhance the patient outcomes. Along with deep learning technology, Machine learning algorithm’s
capacity for pattern recognition has increased significantly, and for some tasks, AI technology’s capacity
for deciphering information patterns has surpassed that of a typical human aptitude.
    Artificial Intelligence is being employed in diagnostic imaging and with chronic diseases to create
precise and useful products that will help treat the sufferers with illnesses and discover treatment at the
earliest stage. The former technology has significant advantages over traditional approaches to health-
care decision-making and analytics. AI algorithms understand training data as they process it, improv-


                                                                                                          117
                                                                                                            ﻿
                                                    Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




ing system accuracy. Humans are now able to gain insights on treatment variability, care procedures,
diagnostics, and patient care that were previously impossible.
   Forecasts from Acumen conclude the study that by 2026, the worldwide economy for AI in the
healthcare sector will be worth US$8 billion.
   Let’s now examine some of artificial intelligence’s current applications in the medical industry.

Accurate Identification of Cancer

Clinicians can make accurate diagnoses with Path AI. As shown in the Figure 1, PathAI which lowers
errors in the cancer diagnostic process and provides a variety of newer methods for customized medical
care. Numerous lives may be saved if cancer patients were diagnosed more accurately so that the major-
ity of them could be treated or cured before it becomes fatal.

Robot-Assisted Surgery

Robot - assisted surgery has become immensely popular. Robotic technology is being used by certain
health facilities helping them to complete activities that call for precision, supervision and versatility. It
is used for tasks like cardiac surgery that are beyond the capabilities of humans.
    The knowledge, skills, and experience of the surgeons are improved by the use of surgical robots
that are furnished with robotic arms, camera, and other necessary tools. Facilitating doctors to control
the robot’s arms from a computer display while the robot provides a magnified, three-dimensional vi-
sion of the surgical site which is impossible through visual inspection as depicted in Figure 2. Artificial
intelligence-assisted surgery with robots result in fewer complications, comparatively reduced pain for
the patients, and shorter recovery times (Lee, 2020).
    The extreme precision, controllability, and flexibility of robotic surgery make it the favored method
for surgeons. Doctors can deal with incredibly complex or previously impossible treatments with robotic
surgery. During surgery, real-time data coupled with medical records enables specialists to see more
patient’s information instantly. By doing this, they can benefit from AI systems that have successfully
utilized data from comparable procedures with success. (Safari, 2019).

Virtual Nursing Assistants

In the patient rooms of Cedars-Sinai Hospital in Los Angeles, California, Amazon’s Alexa robots act as
virtual nurse aides (Johnson, 2017). Alexa assists patients with their daily tasks by performing the tedious



Figure 1. AI for pathologists




118
﻿
Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service



Figure 2. Robot-assisted surgery




tasks assigned to nurses; other patients will take their medicines and become busy with appointments,
and also offer medical advice as shown in the Figure 3.
   The robot helps the doctors and nurses with their sessions of patient visits and provides a list of
hospitalized patients who need to be addressed and managed by the medical staff (Uzialko et al, 2020).
The robot can recognize voices, accompany medical staff to the ward, and translate speech to text to
quickly transcribe electronic medical information.

Care for Rare Diseases

A diagnostic and therapeutic AI-based biomedical platform called BERG aims to map illnesses to speed
up the discovery and development of cutting-edge breakthrough medicines and vaccines, transforming
the way healthcare is delivered. Due to exploratory biology, medical professionals can create durable
products for patients with rare illnesses.
   Additionally, BERG has emphasised their progress in Parkinson’s illness. This neurological disor-
der causes tightness, shakes, and impairments with movement, balance, and other everyday routines.
Parkinson’s disease is one of the worst diseases since its signs and symptoms develop gradually and get
worse with time. BERG connects previously unconnected chemical relationships in the human body
using artificial intelligence.

Automation of Redundant Healthcare Tasks

Administrators can then use their leisure time to work on other crucial and imperative jobs. Olive is a
Intelligence platform that automatically perform a variety of tasks, such as detecting the eligibility of


                                                                                                     119
                                                                                                         ﻿
                                                 Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service



Figure 3. Virtual nursing assistants




adjudicated medical claims and transmitting the necessary medical data to the right medical profession-
als. Olive simply interacts with a hospital’s existing equipment and software, eliminating the need for
pricey downtimes and integrations.

Analysis of Medical Image

Medical disciplines that use images, such as gastrointestinal, ophthalmology, pathology, dermatology
and cardiology, use various machine learning algorithms in medical image analysis and significantly
increases radiology process. Machine learning methods especially use positron emission tomography
(CT), electromagnetic resonance imaging (MRI), ultrasonography, pathological imaging, fundus image,
and endoscope data to identify or categories the severity of the disease.
    For the benefit of chest CT diagnosis, Siemens Health engineers have created AI-based AI-Rad
Companion Chest CT software (Fischer et al, 2020). GE Healthcare is also developing Artificial intel-
ligence technology based medical image analysis.


OPPORTUNITIES INVOLVED IN AI APPLICATIONS IN HEALTHCARE

The current expectation from the market is to construct systems aligned to specific field using machine
learning algorithm with training instances that incorporate details of patients in order to improve the
AI system diagnosis accuracy. The greater the volume of learning examples, AI systems will become
more intelligent. AI medical system offers several novel opportunities to diagnose ailments, offer good



120
﻿
Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




care and a righteous patient experience, motivate patients to take part in their own health care routine,
decreases medical fallacy and healthcare expenditure, and increases managerial effectiveness by health-
care providers.
   But the other perspective of AI brings a lot of new issues. Patient details used for analysis brings in
negative impact on job security (Philips, 2018), mistake accountability and confidentiality and safety
threats in cyber space (Coventry, 2018). To make sure wise use of AI and successful endorsement in the
medical sector, it is very important to traverse major pros and cons involved with AI based applications.
   The usage of AI technology in medicine has given rise to a broad spectrum of opportunities. Below
are few critical ones discussed.

Better Patient Participation and Interaction

The extensively used fitness training application for smartphones, Noom, is a software diet application
program on mobile phones that perform as portable diabetes preventive programme. The objective of
this coaching software is to have persistent devotion to the action plan.
    Meticulous disease diagnosis and patient safety is dependent on patient involvement in medical treat-
ment process. Patient involvement and participation should be main goal for healthcare professionals in
order to enhance service quality while also making the patient experience better.
    Most of the patients are not well known about the usage of AI health care systems, more number of
participants can be expected, if they are made aware about the strength for faster and correct diagnosis,
medical costs reduction while attending the physician. Healthcare systems should establish methods to
notify, teach patients and their clan about the gains and hazards of the AI-integrated medical systems
(Reardon, 2019). Patients who are cognizant will use AI integrated medical systems universally.

Enhanced Medical Care

The release of IBM Watson made a critical decision-making point in the information-controlled stream
of medical investigations and attracted common people in usage of leading-edge technology to enhance
patient wellbeing. Dawes et al. (2017) summarized magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) method of cir-
culatory motion supported by Artificial Intelligence algorithm should be employed to diagnose patients
suffering from hypertension and lung disorder. Technologies based on AI plays a predominant role are
in upgrading health care professionals practically in all facets of patient treatment and increasing the
health care services in developing nations.
   The task can be completed in short duration with increased accuracy and by AI software. The continu-
ous usage of AI systems helps substantially to supplement medical personnel job, where the system alerts
the personnel when blunders are encountered in the treatment process. Systems are capable of intimat-
ing minor areas that humans typically skip and help to decrease medical blunders during diagnosis of
the patient. With increased collection of data and medical research makes AI medical software smarter.

Better Service Delivery and Medical Error Reduction

An AI medical system can detect early-stage polyps that most of the gastroenterologists leave out when
analyzing colonoscopy examinations. Therefore, AI system helps physicians in discovering tiny polyps
providing better patient care and decreasing the medical error.


                                                                                                      121
                                                                                                           ﻿
                                                   Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




    A group of researchers at University of Tokyo Medical School built an Artificial Intelligence system
based on optimal algorithms and many other parameters. The usage of novel algorithms resulted in
leading accuracy rate when combined with deep-learning approach was 83.5% for the sample group of
patients (Sato et al., 2019).
    Radiologists are most frequently named medical professionals who are expected to be substituted
by AI (Pesapane et al., 2018). AI system’s performance exceeded radiologists accuracy, therefore, AI
system are good replacements for radiologists. As a result, doctors have time saved which can be used
for peaceful conversations with patients which will standardize the treatment.

Enhanced Operational Effectiveness and Lower Medical Costs

Several diagnostic process can be taken care by AI-support systems without the interference of humans.
Acute leukaemia can be discovered by latest AI algorithm eliminates cost consuming procedures of
analyzing characteristics of the bone marrow structure. The health care treatment procedure with use
of AI is cost effective and quicker.
   AI solutions are not only used in medical field, but they also assist operational improvements. Main-
tenance systems, accounting, and information enquiry are examples of mundane tasks of AI systems,
which be accomplished effectively than human. The effectiveness of operational mechanism can be
elevated by AI-enabled chatbots and robots.

Enhanced Productivity and the Creation of New Jobs

Will robots and Artificial Intelligence eventually replace human work? The first through fourth indus-
trial revolutions in history have seen the development of industrial expansion and the creation of new
occupations even if many manual work were replaced by cutting-edge technologies.
    For instance, the hard copy printing industry has come to an end, which created the scope for digital
editing and typography.
    An eye disease diagnosis using AI system for degeneration and diabetic edoema using transfer learning
is another excellent illustration of the increased productivity increase. In just 30 seconds, an AI system
can finish the diagnosis procedure, recognizing the disease and its stage of progression. Additionally, the
diagnosis accuracy rate was over 95% when compared diagnosis of five professional ophthalmologists.
These examples show how AI systems can increase productivity by lessening the error rates, shortening
treatment and diagnosis times, and looking into ways to expand care offerings that weren’t previously
available.

Lower Medical Costs

The perfect healthcare system would offer the following: information and proof based illness preven-
tion, best-available automation detection and therapy, patient centric, personal attention, and best quality
care from compassionate medical staff. AI system ensures both quality and cost reductions when used
to assist such a perfect healthcare. ABI Research, a research marketing company, published a paper
claiming that by 2021, the US healthcare sector may save $52 billion as a result of clever applications
of artificial intelligence (Lee,2021). Many health centers in US and Israel have implemented AI based
illness prevention programmers, according to ABI Research.


122
﻿
Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




    In these two nations, it is anticipated that count of AI equipment’s to avoid readful ailment (such as
diabetes and high blood pressure) will ascent from 53,000 in 2017 to over 3.1 million by 2021, a 176%
annual increase. AI applications have capacity to significantly lower medical expenditures for both
individual patients and society. Such savings might be used nationally to improve everyone’s quality of
life through disease prevention.


APPLICATION OF AI IN HEALTHCARE: CHALLENGES

AI applications produces opportunities for enhancing people’s day to day life, they also bring in compli-
cations that need to be managed successfully. The challenges in the healthcare sector are huge in number
because human life is on stake. The below mentioned are few of the difficulties.

System Use Accountability

A human was killed on May 7, 2016, when a Tesla Model S autonomous vehicle malfunctioned. It was
necessary to provide an answer to the question of “Who should bear responsibility for the accident?”
If medical personnel employed an AI system and an incident or error happened, who is responsible?
Human-developed machine learning algorithms are the foundation of how AI-based applications operate.
Hospital administration will decide whether to purchase an AI system after considering input from the
medical personnel, consultants, and experts in the field. The procured equipment must be used by medical
professionals to cater patient needs. So, should the health center, medical professionals or manufactured
organization have held responsible for the incident? With various discussion, deliberation with respect
to technical and ethical concerns, it is very arduous to answer the question (Lupton, 2018) mentioned
the importance of creation of moral and ethical behavioral traits of AI that benefit the society rather
than causing harm to it. With increased usage of AI technologies, these systems must be fabricated with
accomplishment adhering to social norms and values. Accountability for negative effects of AI system
has to be truly grounded on social agreements.

The AI Divide

The fact of patient belief in medical professionals differentiates healthcare from other service industries.
This belief can be proven by the placebo effect, which states medical effect happens when a patient
blindly believes a doctor’s medication will treat her illness. Therefore patient-professional trust is im-
portant because it enhances the patient care. The human individual will be part of care delivery process
that build a bonding between AI system rather than real personage, if an AI system takes over the job
of a medical professional.
    Digital technology usage is going to be difficult to individual who has not used before and to trust
AI completely. AI divide might be bridged, if doctor provides confidence to patient that AI system will
ensure to provide better treatment.




                                                                                                        123
                                                                                                           ﻿
                                                   Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




Security and Confidentiality in Cyberspace

AI-based technologies and systems are built on large volume of datasets, this leads to privacy concerns
regarding data collection. Medical records for patients contain personal information, making it challeng-
ing to transfer disease-related information between various platforms. Therefore, developer must align to
the laws which are a major challenge for AI. The distinctive circumstance of patient is ignored because
AI technology decision making is dependent on data gathered by machine learning algorithm which is
of ethical, moral and legal concern. As a result, it is crucial to concentrate on the morals, regulations,
and values that guide people’s behaviour in society and which an AI system should uphold.

Managerial Control Loss

Health care industry in recent past was a place where patient illness is treated by doctor and nurses.
Now, in addition of receiving treatment, healthy lifestyle gains a important role.AI system are capable of
resolving time and space issues. The conventional method of closed health care system is not prevalent.
Preventive medicine is of focus that lies between healthy lifestyle and technology assistance in medica-
tion. For instance, several smartphone-based apps can assist person’s well-being. AI-based apps can
remotely access, diagnose, treat, and monitor patients who are living at home.
    Care providers are relying on the knowledge of consultants and specialists in healthcare. Therefore,
offering individual attention to patients has emerged into team sport including various internal and ex-
ternal experts. As a result, the conventional governance system would be ineffective in hospitals. In the
view of this process administrators may sense the loss of management control in hospitals (Lee, 2018).

The Suffering of Transition, Education Needs, and Loss of Employment

AI system will inspect medical pictures more correctly than humans. There is a talk that Radiography
may become extinct. Adoption of AI system will result in unemployment and losing the current job.
   In future, it is predicted that AI would focus on building stronger relationship between patient and
professionals. The curriculum for the medical institute should be revised to include AI knowledge.AI
systems are dependent on past dataset of patients (Lupton, 2018). Highly trained professional team is
created to cater the patients instantly by adapting to cutting edge technologies.


AI’S SOCIAL AND SAFETY IMPLICATIONS FOR HEALTHCARE

Healthcare AI may present us with a number of unique difficulties. Additionally, non-IT personnel with
little to no knowledge of AI can make the ultimate decisions about whether or not to integrate a technol-
ogy. Increasing public knowledge, dispelling prevalent AI preconceptions, and developing persuasive
communication skills with governments are all important to address this problem and convince them of
the benefits of integrating the technology (Matheny, 2023).
     The issue of information security and privacy presents another obstacle for AI in healthcare.
     Since AI learns from data from past disease instances, steps must be taken to stop this data from
getting into the hands of unauthorised parties. Additionally, a reliable defence against cyberattacks is
required. Although this is a concern across all industries, it is especially important in healthcare because


124
﻿
Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




the sector directly impacts people’s lives and cyberattacks have the potential to be lethal. Alarmists
frequently cite as instances remote hacking of a cardio stimulator andpurposeful”retraining”ofadiagn
osticandrecommendationsystemtoproposealethalmedication or operation. This could, in a worst-case
scenario, result in mass slaughter.
    Wearable technology must therefore be secured from outside threats. However, there are a number
of issues that need to be addressed, including who determines what protection is dependable and who
will be held accountable in the even to an occurrence (Habli, 2018).
    We also need to consider how AI diagnostic systems will impact all of the people who are still work-
ing in medicine as specialists in terms of their jobs and personal life. There may be two positioning
options (Herrle, 2018).
    Therefore, AI serves as a helper, and the doctor is responsible for evaluating and applying the findings
of its work. If we choose the second option, we must conduct study to determine how much a doctor can
actually be replaced by sophisticated technology. Clearly, there is a concern with the quality of services
that AI may offer and their dependability as doctors. There is also the issue of job loss. For instance,
The Guardian reports that the Japanese company Fukoku Mutual Life Insurance, which specializes in
health insurance, announced the termination of 34 employees in January2017.
    Nevertheless, the layoff of 34 employees was justified by the fact that, from a technological stand-
point, considerable work remains to be done in the areas of privacy and safety, legal obligations, and
health insurance, ethical and psychological concerns. If we build on all of these ideas, AI will develop
into a fantastic tool for preserving and improving human life on a daily basis.


AI IN INDIA HEALTHCARE SECTOR: ITS USAGE, BENEFIT
AND LEVEL OF ACCEPTANCE BY PROFESSIONALS

By 2025, it is anticipated that India would spend $11.78 billion on AI. By 2035, it is projected to have
increased India’s economy by $1 trillion. The massive increase in investment, especially in AI, is taking
place for a variety of reasons. Compared to the global average of 150 doctors per 100,000 people, India
only has 64 doctors available for every 100,000 people. Primary healthcare facilities are underdeveloped
in rural areas. Since more than 70% of the country’s population lives in rural areas, there is a dearth
of high-quality diagnostic services there. The impact of technology has been minimal, even in urban
regions. Online medical consultations saw a significant increase during COVID-19, but the effect has
not been as revolutionary as anticipated. (Selvaraj, 2023).

Result and Analysis

The Benefits of Using AI in the Indian Healthcare Sector

In India, AI grew by more than 109% in 2018, growing to a $665 million industry. By 2025, it is antici-
pated to reach $11.78 billion, which will boost India’s economy by $1 trillion by 2035. According to
estimates, the market for artificial intelligence in healthcare would grow at a CAGR of 37% from 2022
to 2030, from a 2021 value of US$ 11.06 billion to US$ 187.95 billion by 2030 as shown in Figure 4.




                                                                                                        125
                                                                                                          ﻿
                                                  Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service



Figure 4. AI market for artificial intelligence




Leading Healthcare Organizations Using Artificial Intelligence in India

Artificial intelligence can’t only be a curiosity for India, a nation with over 1.3 billion people and a
dire need for medical professionals. India only produces50,000 doctors annually, which is well short
of the required minimum. By 2030, India will require 2.3 million doctors in order to reach the WHO-
recommended minimum doctor-patient ratio of 1: 100. Some of the leading AI organizations working
in the healthcare sector from among the many that are active in the field. The funding received by these
organizations and the research they have published are the two factors used to score them.
    The organizations shown in Figure 5 are the best according to the criteria, according to research and
investigations (Kang, 2023). There is a lot more progress being made in the field of AI, and since the
pandemic Covid-19 expanded to India, many businesses, including hospitals like Apollo Hospital and
Sawai Man Singh Hospital, are incorporating it into their operations. The potential for AI in India is
enormous, making the struggle among different firms to retain or even improve their standing exceed-
ingly difficult.


CONCLUSION AND FUTURE SCOPE

Innovation is much more necessary than ever in the dynamic digital age. The rapidly expanding technol-
ogy is the primary tool for bringing real worth ideas into practice and connecting them together. Because
of the cutting-edge nature of healthcare, the field is especially susceptible to the special application
capabilities of AI. AI-based applications are changing not just how healthcare is provided in terms of
the treatment and diagnosis processes, but also how patients lead their lives since their well-being de-
mands the full spectrum of healthy living habits. We looked into the ways in which AI technology has
permeated the healthcare sector with fresh potential and challenges it has brought. Effective management



126
﻿
Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service



Figure 5. Organizations in healthcare in India




of these opportunities and challenges necessitates the joint knowledge and tenacity of all healthcare
industry stakeholders.

Implications of AI Applications in Healthcare

With the medical staff’s monopolistic power, health services is no longer delivered in a separate shell. A
patient’s health is influenced by a variety of factors, including a healthy diet, physical activity, managing
physical and mental stress, preventive services, and the treatment of illness (Scherer, 2023). Nowadays,
a lot of patients use web resources to conduct their own study into the circumstances affecting their
health or ailments. The job of medical practitioners is regularly supported by AI as well as other digital
tools. A complete view of the dynamic healthcare environment is needed in order to evaluate the trends
of modern technological solutions, as well as the opportunities and issues that these changes bring to
bear(Poly,2023).
    Before adopting these systems, it is crucial to gather and evaluate records of many kinds (such as
patient racial and ethnic traits), as the machine learning technique in AI-based systems necessitates a
significant amount of information for an accurate diagnosis. AI has made considerable inroads into the
medical sector, and this tendency is projected to accelerate in the future, despite the fact that there are
both positive and negative difficulties related to its application and its many elements(Morris, 2023).
    Thus, there is a goal to increase AI research, transparency, and pragmatic use in the healthcare indus-
try. Here are a few tactics that, in our opinion, can be used to manage healthcare-related AI applications
(Darwiesh, 2023).




                                                                                                         127
                                                                                                           ﻿
                                                   Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




    Establishing legal regulations for data collection and management for AI applications is the first step
in the strategy. To improve AI performance in the healthcare sector, real-time data sharing and gathering
information are needed. Because “higher data quality increases users’ confidence in the outputs they
produce, minimizing risk in the results and boosting efficiency” data quality is crucial. Healthcare orga-
nizations should prioritise data quality improvement projects to ensure the reported values are genuine
and reliable. Because symptoms vary based on human traits such racial and ethnic background, way
of life, and economic condition of the patient’s neighbourhood thereby the qualitative accumulation of
precise and realistic data is essential. As a result, suitable data access criteria and mechanisms should
be designed, taking into account the benefits and hazards of data sharing, to improvise the availability
of top-notch information at the national level. Additionally, it is necessary to follow legal guidelines
to safeguard the private information of every patient and to only utilize it collectively for public good.
    Second, Key AI challenges including data exchange, liability, and secrecy require social agreement. It
is important to encourage public participation in order to collect information as it is a vital resource for
medical AI. Social agreement is also needed on the quality control of AI-based systems and liability for
any potential misdiagnosis or medical errors that may occur while providing treatment. Despite wide-
spread worries about the loss of personal information, there is still no social agreement on who should
control the data and how it should be shared, and technology has led to medical mistakes.
    Third, the collaboration of professionals in a number of pertinent domains is required for the applica-
tion of AI-based systems for care services. The medical AI solution’s success or failure will depend on
how closely collaboration with medical field expertise, nursing staff, physicians, mental health workers,
chemists, and patients from the development stage to study and assess the technologies being focused.
Therefore, a collaborative environment must be created for the creation, application, and analysis phases
of AI application.
    Fourth, efforts should be made to make the best use of manpower in innovative value-creating sectors
rather than concentrating primarily on the potential job-destroying impacts of AI systems. Automation
will create new kinds of work, much as what happened during the Industrial Revolution, even though
robots are performing some tasks that humans have previously undertaken. Healthcare businesses ought
to seize the chance to reassign displaced workers to better-paying employment. For instance, the number
of radiologists may decline if AI-based technologies are employed more frequently for diagnostics and
processing medical images. Afterward, radiologists can transition into positions such as medical techni-
cians, who are far more beneficial employees in hospitals. Because they can combine medical science
with ICT. To provide a platform for exchanging and analyzing massive amounts of healthcare data,
the government, ICT industry, and medical community should work together to do studies on medical
informatics.

Limitations and Needs for Future Research

It is projected that the 5G network would accelerate the use of AI in the market for healthcare services
in the foreseeable future. For instance, medical imaging equipment, including MRIs, generates files with
a very big capacity. Advanced networks can raise the calibre and accessibility of medical services by
enabling the quick and precise transport of high-capacity medical picture data. Patients can consult with
specialists right away because the 5G network can be utilised for healthcare on networks without a LAN
connection. In circumstances where the future is uncertain, quick action is the most efficient method to




128
﻿
Artificial Intelligence in Medical and Healthcare Service




reduce damage. AI-based technology is removing time and space barriers and reinventing healthcare,
wellbeing, and perhaps life itself by going beyond the boundaries of expert knowledge.
   In this regard, it is anticipated that the scope of AI-based medical systems would eventually expand
from pre-disease through treatment and post-care to daily living, even though they currently focus on
patient-centered management of diseases. As a result, every part of our everyday lives and every aspect
of healthcare are being impacted by AI-enabled technologies. It should therefore be used from a wide
perspective that takes into account all facets of people’s daily life.
